Transcrição Completa: Scale Your Business Money Making Potential with Shawn Henry
[00:00]
All right, cool. So, few things that I wanted to talk about um both that tie into the agency stuff and fundamentally have changed the way that I look at how I make money and how quickly I make money.
[00:15]
Um you know, for a long time there I always felt like I could be moving more quickly, but I didn't have a framework in which I could actually accomplish that. Um you know, it was like kind of like random. I didn't have like a a mental model, so to speak, on how I could make more money more quickly.
[00:35]
Um, both that contributed to both my agency and, you know, my clients being able to make more money to where our fastest now that we've gone from a completely new high ticket offer to a million a month has been two months.
[00:50]
Um, just like completely fresh like new offer to a million a month in two months. And the way that we accomplish that is the same way that I was able to then now scale up trading to where my biggest month has now been 750,000.
[01:08]
That's all profit by the way, obviously pre-taxes, but again, that's just where I was able to do that in a very short amount of time without leveraging any of my capital, right?
[01:20]
And so, to be clear, it all comes from the same framework. And I I I say this with a lot of like, you know, I'm not going to get up here and maybe get as animated as Jeremy in some ways, but I I actually feel this in a very deep way because I am the first inner circle member.
[01:38]
And I I know a lot of people that have been here for a while that just that have not necessarily progressed at the speed in which I know they want to progress, but also in the speed in which like I would have just quite frankly expected them to at the end of the day.
[01:55]
Because I've definitely had my shortfalls, you know, my shortcomings and and my my pitfalls, but at the end of the day, you should over a long period of time end up farther.
[02:05]
And so I'll talk about the first thing that I changed um both in my own life and in my business is I do this independently. So I do this individually, but this is also something that I have my team do.
[02:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Time Collapsing Exercise"
NÚMEROS: 7 dias, 90 dias
VISUAL: Framework visual mostrando compressão de tempo, conceito de acelerar resultados de 90 dias para 7 dias, representação gráfica de colapso temporal
[02:20]
Um it came about because I wanted to figure out kind of, you know, I I had heard a few people talk about that it's not just about making money, it's about collapsing time, right?
[02:30]
You don't want to make a million a month or a million dollars, you know, a day, like whatever that is. You don't want to get to that point over the course of 50 years or 60 years. You want to do that as quickly as possible, right?
[02:43]
Like, you want to do that as quickly as possible. And so, I came up with the time collapsing exercise that I'm about to show you.
[02:50]
Some of you will listen to this and you will hear it and you will say, "Oh, okay. That sounds pretty cool." And then you might do it, but you won't fully actually embrace it.
[03:00]
And I can promise you for those of you that start using and deploying this at whatever level you're at right now. I don't care what level you're at right now that you'll be able to make more money more quickly.
[03:12]
Some of you will just kind of brush it off though. And I know that just statistically. I've talked to a lot of people about this. But some of you will really lean into this and I will see some crazy like I mean some crazy [ __ ] results in literally a week from now if you actually apply this.
[03:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "7-Day Time Collapsing Exercise"
NÚMEROS: 7 dias, 90 dias
VISUAL: Título principal em destaque, framework estruturado, conceito de exercício semanal de compressão temporal
[03:30]
So it's called the 7-day time collapsing exercise. Everything I talk about is very simple, by the way. It sounds very like, "Oh, yeah, no shit." But, uh, I try to make everything as simple as possible.
[03:42]
The 7-day time collapsing exercise. And it goes like this. First and foremost, you set a very aggressive 90-day goal.
[03:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Step 1: Set Aggressive 90-Day Goal"
NÚMEROS: 90 dias
VISUAL: Primeiro passo destacado, ênfase em meta agressiva trimestral, layout de processo sequencial
[03:52]
You need to know exactly at the end of the quarter where you want to be. Not like where you think you can be, but where you want to be.
[04:02]
And like I talked about, the goal is to collapse time. And in order to do that, you have to think outside the realm of who you are right now and outside of the realm of like the time limitations that you have put on yourself right now in order to get there.
[04:17]
Who's following? Yeah. Make sense?
[04:20]
90 days. You said 90 days.
[04:22]
Yeah. You have to be very clear where you want to be in 90 days.
[04:26]
Can you hear me?
[04:27]
Cool. Yeah. I I can always speak up if you need it, too.
[04:31] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Critical Question"
NÚMEROS: 7 dias para alcançar meta de 90 dias
VISUAL: Pergunta principal em destaque, conceito de urgência extrema (gun to head), cenário hipotético de vida ou morte
[04:33]
Cool. And so once you're clear on the 90-day goal, you do this either every Sunday. So usually what I do is I do this every Sunday with myself and every Monday I have the team do this.
[04:45]
Every single major team member, even like smaller team members, I put through this exercise. And this has changed the way that we do business, how quickly we achieve results.
[04:55]
I asked myself if I had seven days to actually achieve this goal like gun to my head like I would get [ __ ] killed like merked at the end of this seven days if I did not achieve this goal how would I actually achieve it.
[05:12]
Like not what is the stepping stone so I'm on track for 90 days and at the end of this seven days like how do I incrementally make sure that at the end of the seven days I can project out that I'll hit my goal no.
[05:25]
The answer is how do I creatively approach this week to actually accomplish a 90-day goal in 7 days.
[05:33] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Results from Time Compression"
NÚMEROS: $0 para $1M/mês em menos de 2 meses, $200K/mês para $2M/mês em menos de 6 meses

[05:55]

[06:10]
And I know it sounds very simple, but for a lot of you that may feel like you're stuck. It's because you aren't focus. You're focusing on the money, but you're not focusing on how you creatively need to approach the next goal that you have to do it more quickly.
[06:27]
Because the reality is the same way of thinking that you have right now will not enable you to hit your goal.

NÚMEROS: 2 partes: Mindset + Skillset

[07:28]

[07:42]
And this is the idea that every visible result that you want, there is a threshold that is made up of two parts. It's mindset and skill set.
[07:52]
Sounds easy, right? But it's actually a lot more applicable than you think. There is a threshold to visible results for every goal that you want to hit. And it's made up of two parts. Mindset and skill set.
[08:05] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Mindset = What You Believe / Skillset = What You Know"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Definições claras separadas, equação de identidade, fórmula de crenças + conhecimento = identidade
[08:07]
Mindset is what you believe. Your beliefs and your skill set is what you know. And in a combination with what you believe and what you know, you become somebody, right? That's your identity.
[08:20]
And as a result of holding that identity for long enough, consistently enough, you create a baseline. Then you will see the visible results that come out of that.
[08:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "The Identity Hack"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Perguntas-chave destacadas, processo de auto-análise, framework de transformação de crenças
[08:32]
So, if you want to hack, no matter what level you're trying to reach at the next point, like again, I don't care if you're trying to build a 9 figure company or not, or literally you're just trying to hit your first 100k month.
[08:45]
The question that you have to ask yourself is from the mind from the side of mindset, what beliefs do I need to get rid of and what do I need to add that are that are reflective of that person or that goal that I want to have?
[09:00]
Because without that, you it's not like, oh, it'll take longer to hit it. It's just it'll you'll never [ __ ] hit it ever.
[09:08]
Results are a effect. They're not a cause. And again, some of you have heard this before. I'm just trying to tell you like this is like every time my team goes to hit a new goal, we literally talk about this every time.
[09:20]
Okay, what are the beliefs that need to change in order for us to hit this goal? And I go through and I fix this with every single team member on my team in order to do that.
[09:30]
And it becomes that becomes a new standard. If they don't rise to that, then they get cut. It's the same thing with myself. I have to ask myself that.
[09:38]

[09:50]
Then it's just a game of holding that for as long as you can and that's practice right it's not like you snap your fingers unless you get hypnotized if you have all of those things and you get hypnotized that that's actually an asset.
[10:05]

[12:05]

[16:24]
Okay. So when you charge 6,500, everything is relative, right? So when you tell somebody that you charge 6,500 bucks, you think they group you in with everybody else that's charging around that price, even if it's 3K, it's like, well, that's just 3,500 more than 3K.
[16:40]
How much do you think they start to kind of group you in with everybody else? Like you look at all the cars that are between like call it like 50,000 and 150,000 and their value because of their price point is pretty pretty grouped in.
[16:55]
Like you kind of like you're like yeah it's the same caliber of car and then you start to get into the 200,000s. Then you see the cars that are now going for 500k or a million dollars and you start to realize wow those cars must have something special.
[17:08]
Not necessarily true, just to be clear, but their price point means something.
[17:13]
Does your price point mean something at all in the marketplace? When I say my services are $50,000 a month, does that group me in with all the people that are charging 10K, 5K, 6K? No.
[17:25]

[20:32]

[21:00]

[21:08]

[21:24]

[21:38]
Um the first thing that I'd start with is finding the key parts of your business that create results. So, usually it's sales, marketing, and delivery, right?
[21:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Framework-Based Training"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Conceito de simplificação máxima, frameworks vs processos completos, analogia de pintura artística
[21:50]
What I did was I broke each of those parts into as simple of frameworks as I possibly could in order to allow results to be replicatable.
[22:00]
Because it's very difficult. It's very very difficult to train people on entire processes because it's it's like it's almost equivalent to like if I showed you like I was an artist and I showed you a painting that I had painted and I'm like, "All right, here's the painting. Go and make that."
[22:15]
It's not going to work, right? Because you're seeing the macro. You're seeing all of the macro. Even though I could tell you exactly how I did it, each brush stroke, it doesn't matter until you have frameworks that people can grow into.
[22:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sales Framework Simplification"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Princípios de vendas simplificados, conceito de "fazer funcionar até para pessoa não talentosa", framework de autoridade e less is more
[22:30]
So, I broke down the main things for myself, for sales. What are the main frameworks that I do that allow me to close deals, principles, belief systems in order for me that for me to do what I do? I made it as simple as humanly possible.
[22:45]
Literally like like my whole goal that I came up with with the time compression when I had done that exercise is I need to make this to where I can put a really like not a highly talented individual in here but like really really dumb but them still be able to at least produce minimum results.
[23:05]
So for sales it was like okay I'm looking at like the the very simple frameworks that like allow me to close deal what I think allows me to close deals versus somebody else.
[23:15]
Okay, for me like and again I'm not a sales expert. I just tried to like break it down. So for me it was like okay I look at a deal I make sure that I set the frame for authority.
[23:25]
I make sure that they're preframed in this and then with anybody that has sales conversations less is more. So I just I have them talk about le and this is just like this is what I broke down.
[23:35] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Marketing Framework: Zero to One vs One to Infinity"
NÚMEROS: 0→1 (launch to first profitable sales), 1→∞ (scaling)
VISUAL: Duas fases distintas claramente separadas, processo linear de crescimento, definições de cada fase
[23:37]
Same thing with marketing. With marketing, I have very simple framework. Zero to one and one to infinity.
[23:45]
Zero to one is from zero to a new launch for a new client to their first consistent profitable sales that you can predictably produce.
[23:55]
And then one to infinity, which is scaling, like taking initial revenue-driven actions and predictably repeating those over and over and over again at scale.
[24:05]
So I created individual flowcharts and frameworks that went into that that allowed them to start thinking and following that.
[24:13]
So I already took people that knew the system or knew marketing and then I would plug them in and I'd have them everything you do you follow this at each step and they would follow that.
[24:25]
I broke everything into frameworks and ways to think instead of focusing on like teaching them skills.
[24:32]
Teaching skills is like okay we could give them a library of stuff to learn from there. It's just how do you actually apply that?
[24:39]
And so again, I just break things down into the simple way of thinking. It's like the seven-day time compression. Sounds very stupid, like stupid easy, right? But it works.
[24:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "COO Role & Responsibilities"

VISUAL: Estrutura hierárquica com COO no topo, papel de garantidor de frameworks, transição de executor para estrategista
[24:52]
And so that's what I did. And so then I took that and hired a COO. That was one of the biggest things that I did.
[25:00]
She she does things uh much better than I do when it comes to execution. Um but what I was able to do is I gave her all these frameworks and she just she doesn't have to know how to do everything, but she makes sure those frameworks are followed. That's her goal.
[25:18]
Then over time she was able to know the frameworks well enough and worked with the team enough to where she became a savage marketer too. But it was literally like I tried to simplify things as much as possible with that.
[25:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Agency Structure"
NÚMEROS: 72 horas, base semanal, menos de 15 clientes
VISUAL: Organograma completo: CMO → Chief Media Buyer + Chief Copywriter, KPIs por camada, sistema de diagnóstico de red flags
[25:32]
For our agency we have a CMO um he oversees macro data like their specific health KPIs for each client on a 72-hour basis and a week-to-week basis that he is checking in on to see if there's any red flags.
[25:48]
He'll go in if there are any red flags whether it's zero to one or one to infinity processes he'll go in and he'll diagnose it and fix it macro make sure that that's executed.
[26:00]
Underneath him he has a chief media buyer we have a chief copywriter they both individually have their things that they're looking at in terms of KPIs to hit and standards to hit all within simple frameworks.
[26:13] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Copywriting 2-Step Framework"
NÚMEROS: 2 perguntas, 100% yes or no
VISUAL: Framework ultra-simples, duas perguntas principais destacadas, sistema binário de aprovação (hell yes ou hell no)
[26:15]
For copywriting it's super easy we have a two-step framework for producing successful marketing um I know it's an oversimplification but it's it's true like it's just the speed and how how often we can produce high high level marketing.
[26:30]
So the first is is it so simple that a complete stranger would understand and is it boring? It's either a hell yes or a hell no.
[26:40]
We just run every piece of copy, every piece of creative, every piece of every video script runs through that framework. And if it's it's a hell yes or a hell no.
[26:50]
If it's not 100% out of 100%, yes, it's super simple. Even a stranger would understand. And no, it is not boring. If it doesn't hit that, then we redo it until it is.
[27:02]
And that's and that's helped our efficiency as far as marketing results go up. We can hit CTR like make sure that initial launches are hitting CTR within their first launch almost every time just by doing that.
[27:15]
From there, obviously, we're executing that with headlines and the rest of the funnel. So, super simple.

[27:35]

[32:09] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Mental Block on Delegation"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Questão sobre dificuldade de delegar, analogia com trading: green hoje vs lucrativo para sempre
[32:11]
I think mentally a lot of us or maybe I'm projecting um have the issue of even if we know we're not actually the best at doing every little piece, we find it really hard to delegate. How have you adjusted your mind around delegating pieces either the ones that aren't very high value ones that are?
[32:30]
Yeah. So, I'll kind of bring it back to like trading, right? Trading I I I've tried to I've I've taught a few people, you know, I've probably I've taught this quite to to enough people to where I've seen who succeeds and who doesn't.
[32:45]
Um, but the main reason people fail at trading is because they care more about seeing green today than being profitable for the rest of their life.
[32:55] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Green Today vs Profitable Forever"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Comparação de mentalidade curto prazo vs longo prazo, aplicação ao negócio: metas atuais vs riqueza vitalícia
[32:57]
And I say this to everybody that I train. Do you care more about being green today or do you care more about being profitable for the rest of your life?
[33:05]
What you're saying is, you know, you know, I don't feel fully comfortable tra you know, doing X, Y, and Z.
[33:12]
The the thing you have to ask yourself is would I rather be green today which is meaning even if I made mistakes somehow getting back up to where I needed to be or would I rather be profitable for the rest of my life.
[33:25]
But in your case like for the business it's would I rather hit my goals that I want to hit or would I rather stay basically green and safe for here for today or would I rather be as profitable or as rich as I want to be for the rest of my life.
[33:40]
The results that you want are a reflection of the identity that you will have in the future. So, in order for you to do that, you have to focus more on the identity and less on these actions because they're all a reflection of your identity.
[33:55]
So, spend some time with the beliefs and and kind of play around with that. But everyone's journey to overcoming those beliefs are different.
[34:03]
Um, but I would set a very ruthless standard for cutting those out. Like I would like the same ruthlessness you would give somebody else that's executing on specific tasks, you have to give yourself that same degree of ruthlessness to cut out the negative beliefs that are keeping you from actually getting where you want to be.
[34:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Breaking Binary Thinking"
NÚMEROS: Não especificado
VISUAL: Paradigma preto e branco: "eu faço melhor" vs "eles fazem subpar", necessidade de quebrar essa mentalidade
[34:24]
Because then you're like, well, I want to have I want to eventually delegate these things. But the reality is um you will never come up with the creative answer to actually have them delegating better than you if you're forced into a paradigm where it's either black or white.
[34:40]
Either I do it the best or they do it subpar. That's that's your frame. That's your frame.
[34:46]
Right. Correct. It's it it's not it's not exactly what you're looking for.
[34:51]
And so challenge and face those beliefs that are around that. like really get clear on like if I want to hit this goal, what is the threshold to result in terms of skill set and mindset that I need to do in order to hit that?
[35:05]
And then you'll be able to hit that more quickly because if you spend your time focusing on that instead of you know obsessing over the number or obsessing over the next action like the micro action like delegating then everything becomes very clear and a new standard gets set.
[35:22]
Yeah, what's up?

[39:53]
Uh stock trading.
[39:55]
Stock trading. So the my my system is very it's like again as simple as possible.
[40:02]
Um there are specific indicators also like estimated moving averages in combination with um a dynamic indicator that I found that plots support and resistance.
[40:13]
And I only play rejections. So, I play the two-minute ca candles and uh I play the open when volatility is the highest.
[40:23]
I watch for these initial moves that then go and reject against these points as support and resistance. So, they might go up like five points on NASDAQ, reject down, and then that's where I take my like.
[40:35]
For example, if it's going up and it rejects down, that's where I take my short and then I'll put it uh put my stop loss above the that candle high and then I'll ride it down or up depending on that.
[40:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Risk-to-Reward Trading"

[42:42]

[42:55]

[43:05]

[43:15]

[43:25]

[45:20]
Yeah. And I just have a 9010 split with them and then I've spund as much as possible. So, I'm trading with about three million in capital.
[45:30]
I was just curious if you had your own fun structure or something like that.
[45:34]
No, but that's what Jeremy is saying, you know, like cuz I want to do $100 million a year trading to be clear and I know I can do it.
[45:42]
Uh I just have to find where the capital is in order to get to that next level. So I need to go from basically trading like 3 to 5 million in capital to going and trading like 60 to 70 million for other people.
[45:55]
Yeah. So that's the plan. Yeah. That's the next step.
[45:59] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Type Form Conditional Logic"
NÚMEROS: 2-3 pontos de fricção
VISUAL: Qualificação financeira + comprometimento, perguntas longas vs múltipla escolha, law of diminishing returns
[46:01]
In your type form when you do the conditional logic are you doing just based on reported revenue or income or do you do you also layer it for a few different questions?
[46:12]
Yeah, I usually put two points of friction. Income and revenue is one. And then usually in terms of commitment because it's either it's either financial qualification or commitment like commitment to the process and engaging in the sales conversation like that kind of qualification.
[46:30]
And so I'll usually put like some kind of long form question in there to ask to have them type something in because if they type something in even if it's one word that's more commitment than somebody answering a multiple choice.
[46:45]
And then um and then a financial question. That's where I'll start. If they're still not qualified, then I'll add a third point of friction. But that's that's usually where I start.
[46:55]
But how would you filter out that that uh open-ended question or whatever? Like
[47:00]
That's just that's just f that's just interest. It's not I'm not filtering out. The only thing I'm filtering in that case like actually filtering is uh the financial question.
[47:10]
For correct correct but in but it's like think about it at least perspect like my perspective again it's an oversimplification but you have to start somewhere and then you can adjust based off the data for each each client.
[47:23]
But like in that case like start with the financial question. It's either financial qualification or interest qualification.
[47:30]
Like they have to be like somewhat like more committed or interested to the sales process to actually engage in the sales conversation to then be led down the sales process to be able to get that to get the better leads.
[47:43]
And so for us adding that long form that actually causes like them to think and commit plus that is it's like law of diminishing returns.
[47:52]
We're not adding too much friction without having data to support it. And we're not we we have enough friction to get those people out. But I I don't want to add too much especially when I'm watching.
[48:05]
Filtering them out to for feedback based on their answer to that open-ended question.
[48:10]
No.
[48:11]
Just out of curiosity, is it an indicator you're using open?
[48:15] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Trading Indicators"
NÚMEROS: EMAs: 9, 21, 50, 200
VISUAL: Não usa VWAP, indicador dinâmico customizado, Trading View → Ninja Trader
[48:17]
No, I don't use VWAP. I I just use I I play around with it. Um, usually it's like 921 50 200 pretty standard. Um, yeah, just the EMAs.
[48:30]
But then I, like I said, I have another indicator that I found that before I was plotting my own support and resistance.
[48:37]
Uh, but I found this that is a dynamic indicator that I built in originally found it on Trading View and then um, a programmer brought it into Ninja Trader, which is what I use as my I guess whatever you the [ __ ] you call it.
[48:52]
Kind of like whatever platform you trade on like IBK or or anything like that. But yeah, that's uh that's what I brought it into and that's all I use to trade.
[49:02]
Keep it pretty simple.
[49:04] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Sales Process Timeline"
NÚMEROS: Semana 1: launch + KPIs, Semana 2: primeiro cost per call lucrativo + primeira venda, Semana 3: escalar
VISUAL: Timeline de 3 semanas, expectativas claras por fase, ênfase em zero to one
[49:06]
Uh how long is your typical sales process for like the 30K package when you're closing on that?
[49:12]
Like how long does it take? Like like a one call close usually. Mhm. And then as far as or or we follow Yeah. Yeah. Like some deals it might take like a couple days.
[49:25]
How long like for the month to month cuz I know it's 3 months you said you correct 90 days.
[49:32]
You guys usually tell them like month one two and three as far as like the expected results like expectations.
[49:40]
Yeah. I mean for for us it's like okay week one we need to make sure the launch like it's hitting KPIs like and I'll give them the exact KPIs that we're trying to hit.
[49:50]
And then like week two we're going for like our first like very profitable cost per calls and and hopefully our first sale because again this time compression that we've set up has allowed us to compress results very quickly.
[50:05]
And then from there huh okay and then from there uh the last part is like okay week three then we're obviously like lining up and you know we'll just we'll just kind of like like basically let them know.
[50:18]
The hardest part is zero to one which is getting your first two p uh predictable profitable sales unless they're already running ads then then it's like okay just let us plug in and you guys are going to be getting almost instantaneous.
[50:32]
So your time collapse framework since implementing it. Can you talk through some of the specific questions you ask yourself about a situation in order to achieve that? Like you can go through a little bit more of that process.
[50:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Time Collapse Thinking Process"
NÚMEROS: 7 dias para meta de 90 dias
VISUAL: Processo de pensamento: meta de 90 dias → mindset + skillset necessários → discrepâncias → ações
[50:47]
Yeah. So it's thinking time. If you haven't deployed it um like in that way, but you're basically saying, hey, if I had seven days, what are the like if I had seven days to accomplish my 90-day goal? Get in my head. I had to do it.
[51:02]
What are the beliefs set? Like what do I need to change in terms of like my mindset and skill set? Break that into two parts.
[51:10]
Start out with limiting beliefs.
[51:12]
Don't focus on limiting beliefs. Just focus on like what beliefs like what like focus on the the goal. The goal define the beliefs that that goal has and see where the discrepancy is.
[51:25]
And and it's it's like these three things I need to these three things I need to get rid of. This one thing I need to add to and just like assess your future self against yourself now. So that you can do that.
[51:38]
Nick, we we'll we'll talk about it after. Jeremy Jeremy wants me to get off.
[51:43]
Yeah. Give it up.
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
$0 para $1M/mês em menos de 2 meses (novo high ticket offer)
Cliente Marcel: $200K/mês para $2M/mês em menos de 6 meses
Trading: maior mês de $750K (puro lucro pré-impostos)
Trading: média de $40-50K por dia em 15 minutos
Trading inicial: $20-30K/mês
Trading com ~$3M em capital (prop firms, split 90/10)
Meta trading: $100M/ano
Precificação evoluiu de $10K/mês para $30-50K/mês + upfront + rev share
Marcel (hipnotista): $1M/ano por 4 horas/mês
Cold outreach: início 100-200/dia, atual 20-50/dia
Novo produto: $30K por 90 dias
Ads performance: ROI 25x
3 deals fechados a $30K com budget baixo
Marcel: 2 clientes de $1M/ano fechados em 1 semana (ambos $100M+ net worth)
Meta da agência: $1B/ano em vendas para clientes (precisa 10x resultado atual)
Menos de 15 clientes ativos
COO: começou $1K/mês, depois $4K/mês, equity ao fim do ano
KPIs checados a cada 72 horas e semanalmente
2 CSMs (VAs das Filipinas)
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
7-Day Time Collapsing Exercise:
Definir meta agressiva de 90 dias
Toda semana (domingo para si, segunda para equipe)
Pergunta central: "Se eu tivesse 7 dias para alcançar minha meta de 90 dias (gun to head), como faria?"
Não é sobre stepping stones, é sobre criatividade extrema
Força mudança de pensamento e expansão de identidade
Threshold to Visible Results Framework:
Toda meta tem um threshold de 2 partes: Mindset + Skillset
Mindset = O que você acredita (beliefs)
Skillset = O que você sabe (knowledge)
Mindset + Skillset = Identidade
Identidade sustentada = Baseline = Resultados visíveis
Perguntas-chave: "Que crenças preciso eliminar?" e "Que crenças preciso adicionar?"
"O que preciso saber para estar nesse nível?"
Trading Strategy:
Trade apenas o open (abertura do mercado)
15 minutos por dia, todo lucro nesse período
Velas de 2 minutos
Indicadores: EMAs (9, 21, 50, 200) + indicador dinâmico de suporte/resistência
Estratégia: play rejections apenas
Risk-to-reward favorável (perder 2 + ganhar 1 = positivo)
Ex: NASDAQ sobe 5 pontos, rejeita, entra short com stop loss acima do candle high
Prop firms com split 90/10
Trading View → Ninja Trader (plataforma)
Precificação e Posicionamento:
Foco na dor do cliente, não no seu valor
Price point cria percepção (analogia carros $50K-150K vs $200K-1M)
Sell to richer audience ou analise quanto pagam para sair da dor
Target: negócios fazendo $100K+/mês mínimo
"Seu preço é reflexo da sua identidade"
Se acredita que deve cobrar mais e não cobra = desalinhamento de identidade = meta impossível
Marketing Framework - Zero to One vs One to Infinity:
Zero to One: novo launch até primeiras vendas lucrativas consistentes e previsíveis
One to Infinity: scaling - repetir ações geradoras de receita previsivelmente em escala
Flowcharts e frameworks para cada fase
Copywriting 2-Step Framework:
É tão simples que um completo estranho entenderia?
É entediante?
Hell yes ou hell no (100% ou refaz)
Toda copy, creative e video script passa por isso
Resultado: CTR alto quase sempre no primeiro launch
Media Buying Framework:
Cut first ao escalar
Core campaigns
Sistema 1:3 - cada winner cria 3 novos winners
Cold Outreach:
Sempre ativo
Vídeo pitches
Nicho ampliado: high ticket services (não apenas personal brand)
Incluindo financial services
Paid Ads Strategy:
Messaging insanely direct
Articular diálogo interno do cliente melhor que qualquer um
Falar direto aos problemas específicos
Mostrar big results ($300M gerados)
Garantia forte com confiança
Conditional logic (Type Form)
Pixel nunca vê leads de baixa qualidade
Targeting: $100K+/mês mínimo
Backend automatizado (texting automático, etc)
Type Form Conditional Logic:
2-3 pontos de fricção máximo
Ponto 1: Qualificação financeira (revenue/income)
Ponto 2: Comprometimento (pergunta long-form para digitar)
Digitar = mais comprometimento que múltipla escolha
Apenas financial question filtra de verdade
Long-form é para interest qualification
Law of diminishing returns - não adicionar fricção sem dados
Sales Process:
Estrutura: 1 setter + 2 closers (1 part-time + 1 full-time)
Setter para leads de ads
Part-time também treina clientes em sales
Segmentação inbound vs outbound (livro: Predictable Revenue - Salesforce)
Dominar um canal antes de adicionar outro
One call close geralmente, alguns levam dias
Frameworks simples vs processos completos
Sales: set frame for authority + preframe + less is more
Post-90 Day Options:
Renovar $30K por mais 90 dias
Upgrade para $30-50K/mês (push forte se aceitando partner clients)
Treinar equipe interna (menos eficiente que agência)
Potencial: vender 6 meses ou 1 ano após entregar resultados
Client Onboarding (90 dias):
Semana 1: Launch hitting KPIs específicos
Semana 2: Primeiro cost per call lucrativo + primeira venda (time compression permite)
Semana 3: Scaling e lineup
Parte mais difícil: zero to one (2 vendas lucrativas previsíveis)
Se já roda ads: resultados quase instantâneos
ESTRUTURA:
Team Infrastructure:
Quebrar negócio em 3 partes: Sales, Marketing, Delivery
Criar frameworks mais simples possíveis (não processos completos)
Meta: pessoa não talentosa conseguir resultado mínimo
Frameworks = ways to think, não skills
COO gerencia frameworks, não executa tudo
COO eventualmente domina frameworks e se torna expert
Hierarchy:
CMO (Chief Marketing Officer)
Oversees macro data
Health KPIs por cliente (72h e semanal)
Diagnóstica e fixa red flags
Supervisiona zero to one e one to infinity
Chief Media Buyer (abaixo)
Chief Copywriter (abaixo)
COO (Chief Operating Officer)
Garante que frameworks sejam seguidos
Implementa 7-day time compression na empresa
Roda o negócio inteiro
Métricas trimestrais com bônus
Responsável por performance do cliente
Equity contingente a permanência
2 CSMs (Customer Success Managers - VAs Filipinas)
Avaliam infelicidade ou necessidade de dados do cliente
Sem reuniões recorrentes (apenas sob demanda)
Foco em resultados, não reuniões
COO Journey:
Veio de firma de contabilidade
Começou querendo apenas trabalhar no negócio
Muito seletivo na contratação
Progressão: $1K/mês → $4K/mês → equity
Equity contingente a permanência (X tempo)
Realização de equity provavelmente não será por venda (cash flow agency)
Simbólico de loyalty e long-term commitment
Métricas trimestrais específicas = bônus
Agora roda o negócio inteiro
Philosophy on Delegation:
Analogia trading: "Green hoje vs lucrativo para sempre"
Para negócio: "Atingir metas vs ser rico para sempre"
Resultados são reflexo da identidade futura
Focar na identidade, não nas ações
Mesma ruthlessness para cortar crenças negativas que para cortar funcionários
Quebrar paradigma binário: "eu faço melhor" vs "eles fazem subpar"
Criatividade vem de forçar novo paradigma
Frameworks Philosophy:
Tudo o mais simples possível
Não ensinar skills, ensinar ways to think
Biblioteca de conteúdo separada para skills
Frameworks permitem replicabilidade
Analogia: não mostrar pintura pronta e pedir para replicar
Mostrar frameworks que levam ao resultado
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
Agência: $30-50K/mês + upfront + rev share
Agência antiga: $10K/mês
Trading: $750K maior mês (puro lucro pré-tax)
Trading: $40-50K/dia em 15 minutos
Trading: ~$3M em capital (prop firms 90/10)
Marcel: $1M/ano (4h/mês)
Marcel: 2 clientes de $1M/ano fechados em 1 semana
Novo produto: $30K por 90 dias (versão limitada)
Ads ROI: 25x
Target client: $100K+/mês mínimo
Meta agência: $1B/ano para clientes (precisa 10x atual)
Menos de 15 clientes ativos
COO: $1K/mês → $4K/mês + equity
DURAÇÃO: ~51 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação presencial com Q&A, Inner Circle member training
